question_text: Two triangles overlap and share one side. Which statement would be most useful in proving them congruent?
option_a: A shared side can be used as an equal corresponding side
option_b: The overlap automatically makes the triangles equilateral
option_c: Only the area of the overlap matters
option_d: Shared vertices guarantee similarity but not congruence
explanation: A common side can serve as one of the equal corresponding sides in a congruence argument.
